title: CarPlay Is Additive
author: sprawl_
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: Back in March, Rivian’s Chief Software Officer, Wassym Bensaid, was interviewed by the excellent Nilay Patel for Decoder. If you’re not a Decoder listener, you should be. If you’re not a subscriber of The Verge, you should be. But that’s not what I’m here to talk about. In this interview — which I greatly enjoyed! — Nilay grills Wassym on all manner of issues. The whole time, though, I was waiting for one topic: CarPlay. CarPlay is a way to interact with your phone via your car’s infotainment. Our last three cars, the eldest of which was from the 2017 model year, have all had CarPlay. They make the experience of being in the car way way better. Any of the apps I really care about, and would want to interact with while on the road, have a bespoke CarPlay interface. I literally will not buy a car that does not support CarPlay. Most of the way through the episode, Nilay asked Wassym about CarPlay. The question was long, but the gist is this:

I hear from our readers every time I talk to a car executive that, “The reason I want CarPlay is because there’s 5,000 apps on my phone and no car OEM is ever going to support them in the built-in infotainment.” This is when you would say, “Okay, project your phone to the center stack. The car’s driving itself. Have at it. Phone projection all day.” Do you think the tide is turning, or are you still absolutely committed to not having CarPlay in Rivian vehicles?
You can read Wassym’s full answer at the episode link, but here’s the part that stuck out to me:
The challenge with screen mirroring solutions is that they take over every single pixel in the car, and that’s not the way we see ourselves interacting with our users.

If you’re not a subscriber of The Verge, you should be. But that’s not what I’m here to talk about. In this interview — which I greatly enjoyed! — Nilay grills Wassym on all manner of issues. The whole time, though, I was waiting for one topic: CarPlay. CarPlay is a way to interact with your phone via your car’s infotainment. Our last three cars, the eldest of which was from the 2017 model year, have all had CarPlay. They make the experience of being in the car way way better. Any of the apps I really care about, and would want to interact with while on the road, have a bespoke CarPlay interface. I literally will not buy a car that does not support CarPlay. Most of the way through the episode, Nilay asked Wassym about CarPlay. The question was long, but the gist is this:

I hear from our readers every time I talk to a car executive that, “The reason I want CarPlay is because there’s 5,000 apps on my phone and no car OEM is ever going to support them in the built-in infotainment.” This is when you would say, “Okay, project your phone to the center stack. The car’s driving itself. Have at it. Phone projection all day.” Do you think the tide is turning, or are you still absolutely committed to not having CarPlay in Rivian vehicles?

comments.author: linzhangrun
comments.textRawHtml:
For pure interaction, CarPlay as a generic solution is very hard to beat infotainment systems that are deeply integrated with the vehicle itself. Its advantages are mainly these:

- Consistency. You get into a new car, all you need to figure out is how to open CarPlay, no need to learning a completely different and often complicated infotainment system.

- "It's on your phone". You can decide what playlist to play or where to navigate before you even get into the car.

- Stays up to date over the long term. Just look at cars from five years ago. Most built-in infotainment systems are still stuck in that era, no matter how smart they once looked. CarPlay uses your phone as the main computing platform, and the car's infotainment system only needs to act as a thin client for I/O, it keeps updating with iOS.
comments.textZhHtml:
就纯粹的交互体验而言，CarPlay作为一种通用解决方案，很难被与车辆深度集成的车载信息娱乐系统所超越。它的优势主要体现在以下几个方面：

- 一致性。当你坐进一辆新车时，只需弄清楚如何打开CarPlay即可，无需去学习一套完全不同且往往复杂的车载信息娱乐系统。

- “一切都在手机上”。你甚至在上车前就能决定播放什么歌单或导航去哪里。

- 长期保持更新。看看五年前的车就知道了。大多数内置信息娱乐系统至今仍停留在过去的水平，无论它们曾经看起来多么智能。CarPlay将你的手机作为主要计算平台，车载信息娱乐系统只需充当输入输出的瘦客户端，并随着iOS不断更新。

children.author: rkangel
children.textRawHtml:
The big thing for me is that the lifetime of a car is a lot longer than the technology development lifecycle.

I just bought a 10 year old Toyota estate (station wagon). It's got a reasonable screen and Bluetooth implementation etc. But I'm never going to want to use the built in navigation because it's just not as good as what my phone will do. And the audio integration isn't as sophisticated as it might be - I have to choose the app on my phone.

Whereas CarPlay/AndroidAuto is generic from the car point of view, and as phone features and software improve your car capability evolves too.
children.textZhHtml:
对我来说最重要的一点是，汽车的使用寿命远远长于技术的发展周期。

我刚买了一辆10年车龄的丰田旅行车。它配备了不错的屏幕和蓝牙功能等等。但我绝不会想用它的内置导航，因为它根本比不上手机导航。而且音频集成也不够完善——我必须在手机上选择应用。

而从汽车的角度来看，CarPlay/Android Auto是通用的，随着手机功能和软件的升级，你的车载体验也会随之进化。

children.author: darrylb42
children.textRawHtml: I added a new head unit to my 13 year old Santa Fe, it is like a new car now, wireless Android Auto.  Connects quick, bigger nicer screen.  Should allow me to keep the car for another 10 years assuming I don't blow up another engine, only the first one is free.
children.textZhHtml: 我给13年车龄的圣达菲换了个新中控屏，现在它就像辆新车一样，支持无线Android Auto。连接速度快，屏幕更大更漂亮。只要我不再把发动机搞坏，这车应该能让我再开10年，毕竟只有第一台发动机是免费的。

children.author: titanomachy
children.textRawHtml: I’m sure the car manufacturers would much prefer if you updated your car as often as you do your phone.
children.textZhHtml: 我敢肯定，汽车制造商更希望你换车的频率能像换手机一样高。

children.author: bluGill
children.textRawHtml:
They want everyone to, but they also know that the cost of cars, it's not realistic for that to happen. The only way even people on the upper middle class can afford to be buying or leasing cars every three years is because at the end of those three years the car still has a lot of value left to sell on to someone who is more middle class. And that middle class person will likely sell it again to someone who is a little bit lower down and at about 10 to 12 years it finally reaches someone who is poor.

The only way someone could buy a car more often is if they became a lot cheaper. That would mean doing away with a lot of the luxury parts of the car that are where the profit is.
children.textZhHtml:
他们希望所有人都能这么做，但他们也知道，以现在的汽车成本，这并不现实。即使是中上层阶级的人，之所以能负担得起每三年买一次或租一次车，唯一的原因就是三年期满后，这辆车还有很大的剩余价值，可以卖给更普通的中产阶级。而这个中产阶级的人很可能又会把它卖给收入再低一点的人，到了大约10到12年车龄时，它最终会落到穷人手里。

人们想要更频繁地换车，唯一的办法就是让车价大幅降低。这就意味着要取消汽车上很多用来赚取利润的豪华配置。

children.author: xp84
children.textRawHtml: They’re working on it. I follow a mechanic on YouTube who has shared dozens of examples of manufacturers seemingly intentionally shipping deeply flawed components for seemingly no reason other than to push the average lifespan of a vehicle back downwards from the high levels we’d reached by about 2012. Cars are being junked with less than 100k miles because the original parts fail, replacement parts are hard to get, and now manufacturers are making it so you need an expensive piece of software which they completely control to program a new part to the car, without which, it’s a brick. So essentially they can drop support for a car from that software, and make it near-impossible to replace those parts even if someone reverse-engineers aftermarket parts.
children.textZhHtml: 他们正在这么做。我在YouTube上关注了一位修车师傅，他分享了数十个例子，说明汽车制造商似乎在故意发运存在严重缺陷的零部件，似乎毫无其他原因，只是为了把车辆的平均使用寿命从我们在2012年左右达到的高水平重新拉低。现在有些车行驶里程还不到10万英里就被报废了，因为原厂件坏了，替换件又很难弄到，而且现在制造商还搞出这么一套：你需要用一款完全由他们控制的昂贵软件来给新车编程，否则这车就是一块砖头。所以本质上，他们可以从那款软件中取消对某款车的支持，让那些零件几乎无法更换，即使有人逆向工程搞出了副厂件也没用。

children.author: jaskerr
children.textRawHtml: Care to name the mechanic on YouTube?
children.textZhHtml: 能透露一下那位YouTube修车师傅的名字吗？

children.author: LgWoodenBadger
children.textRawHtml:
This isn’t to whom parent is referring, but you can watch this video about wet timing belts for an idea https://youtu.be/0SASSFjIt5I

In short, it’s an awful idea because even if the belt doesn’t fail early, debris from the belt will clog oil pickups, galleries, and passages.
children.textZhHtml:
这不是楼主所指的，但你可以看看这个关于湿式正时皮带的视频了解一下 https://youtu.be/0SASSFjIt5I

简而言之，这是个糟糕的主意，因为即使皮带没有提前损坏，皮带产生的碎屑也会堵塞机油集滤器、油道和油路。

children.author: swiftcoder
children.textRawHtml: I’m sure they would, but they also know their new car prices would be unsustainable if you couldn’t sell your old car… and that means everyone in the second hand car market is running stale infotainment stacks
children.textZhHtml: 我敢肯定他们愿意，但他们也知道，如果旧车卖不出去，新车价格就难以为继……这就意味着二手车市场上的所有人都在用过时的信息娱乐系统。

children.author: linzhangrun
children.textRawHtml: Current State of Chinese EV Manufacturers
children.textZhHtml: 中国电动汽车制造商现状

children.author: xp84
children.textRawHtml: Source? They’re cheaper, but I’ve seen zero proof they are less long-lasting than our cars. If anything, those who have tried them say they’re better cars, including the Ford CEO, who imported one and can’t say enough good things about it.
children.textZhHtml: 来源呢？它们确实更便宜，但我完全没看到任何证据表明它们不如我们的车耐用。相反，试过的人都说它们的车更好，就连福特CEO也进口了一辆，并且对其赞不绝口。

children.author: HPsquared
children.textRawHtml: Some of which are also phone manufacturers.
children.textZhHtml: 其中一些公司同时也是手机制造商。

children.author: loloquwowndueo
children.textRawHtml: Make them cheaper and I’m game.
children.textZhHtml: 把价格降下来我就买账。

children.author: maxdo
children.textRawHtml: Well, not every car brand suck at that. My 6 y.o tesla still delivers me updates, and new features.
children.textZhHtml: 好吧，也不是所有汽车品牌在这方面都很差。我那辆6年车龄的特斯拉依然能给我推送更新和新功能。

children.author: theshrike79
children.textRawHtml:
To be honest, Tesla is a computer with a car as an add on. That’s why it works.

Traditional companies do it the other way around.
children.textZhHtml:
说实话，特斯拉就是一台电脑，汽车只是它的附加组件。这就是它能成功的原因。

传统车企的做法恰恰相反。

children.author: polarix
children.textRawHtml: I’m not sure it “works”. I would still drastically prefer if Tesla had CarPlay.
children.textZhHtml: 我不确定这是否真的“行得通”。如果特斯拉能支持CarPlay，我会更乐意选择它。

children.author: thek3nger
children.textRawHtml: Another advantage of the "it's on your phone" aspect is that it follows you when you switch cars. If I use my friend's car, I still have MY system. If I rent a car for a trip, I still have MY system.
children.textZhHtml: “一切都在手机上”的另一个优势在于，当你换车时，它能一路跟随你。如果我开朋友的车，我用的依然是“我的”系统。如果我租车出行，我用的也依然是“我的”系统。

children.author: dataengineer56
children.textRawHtml: Another advantage of the "it's on your phone" aspect is Consistency. You get into a new car, all you need to figure out is how to open CarPlay, no need to learning a completely different and often complicated infotainment system.
children.textZhHtml: “一切都在手机上”的另一个优势在于一致性。当你坐进一辆新车时，只需弄清楚如何打开CarPlay即可，无需去学习一套完全不同且往往复杂的车载信息娱乐系统。

children.author: s3p
children.textRawHtml:
>For pure interaction, CarPlay as a generic solution is very hard to beat infotainment systems that are deeply integrated with the vehicle itself

I can't find one instance of a car UI being as good or easier to interact with than CarPlay.
children.textZhHtml:
>就纯粹的交互体验而言，CarPlay作为一种通用解决方案，很难被与车辆深度集成的车载信息娱乐系统所超越

我找不到任何一个车载UI的交互体验能媲美甚至超越CarPlay的例子。

children.author: josefresco
children.textRawHtml: My daughter's very ordinary 10+ year old Kia Soul has a very nice navigation and media UI.  It's not the base model but it's a joy to use because it's incredibly responsive and very well integrated with the car's physical buttons.  The SiriusXM radio even has a built-in DAR!
children.textZhHtml: 我女儿那辆非常普通的、车龄十多年的起亚秀尔，其导航和媒体用户界面却非常棒。它不是基础款，但用起来非常顺手，因为它的响应速度极快，而且与车内的物理按键整合得非常好。SiriusXM收音机甚至内置了DAR！
